name: Black Cohosh Actaea racemosa
summary: Black Cohosh (Actaea racemosa) is most studied for menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, with proposed serotonergic and estrogen-receptor-modulating activity rather than classical hormonal action.
description: Black Cohosh (Actaea racemosa) is most studied for menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, with proposed serotonergic and estrogen-receptor-modulating activity rather than classical hormonal action.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated; rare suspected hepatotoxicity reports; clinical trials have not consistently shown liver injury

sources.title: Black Cohosh: Health Professional Fact Sheet
Black Cohosh
sources.authors: LiverTox: Clinical and Research Information on Drug-Induced Liver Injury

sources.title: Black cohosh extracts in women with menopausal symptoms

dosage: Standardized extract (e.g., 2.5% triterpene glycosides): 20-80 mg/day; variability across products
typical_dosage: Standardized extract (e.g., 2.5% triterpene glycosides): 20-80 mg/day; variability across products
